pages.text: Oyisa loves to eat all kinds of fruit. She eats pears, grapes, oranges, and watermelon.
whole She can eat a lot of fruit in no time at all.
But she doesn’t like the seeds. They get stuck in her teeth Yuck! and taste bitter.
Today, she’s eating a delicious, juicy pear. She spits the seeds out on the ground.
A plant begins to grow between her feet. That’s strange, thinks Oyisa. What’s going on here?
high Suddenly, a tree shoots up – with Oyisa perched on top! Oyisa needs to find a way down.
The branches look like a swing. ”! m ga w i n j i u d ’ m n a n u g i n j i g i n she sings. i J “
swiiiiinggs She to the next branch and meets a bird.
The bird wants to build a cosy nest in her hair. “ N o w a y, M r B i r d! ”
On her way down, she spies a giant pear and climbs onto it. It sways in different directions, and this is the best feeling in the world!
o r f e m t i! ’ s a I t a a s. a a ll s c y i a O m “ a M, ” h a t b u r o y g”! mn i o Imc ’ “ shouts Oyisa from the tree.
What an adventure! Oyisa decides to start collecting seeds. Who knows what magic lies inside them?
